ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate cake as a food item; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared desserts is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of classic chocolate cake is one slice, approximately 80 g (one-eighth of a 6-inch whole cake), providing around 294 kcal based on USDA reference values for chocolate layer cake with frosting.

nutrient_source.title: Cake, chocolate, commercially prepared with chocolate frosting — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 18101)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The Singapore HPB source reports anomalously low macros (energy 42 kcal/100g, protein 0g, fat 0.25g) inconsistent with any known chocolate cake formulation. These values appear to be data entry errors or represent a flavoured beverage/syrup rather than a whole cake. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared chocolate cake with chocolate frosting (FDC ID 18101) is used as the authoritative nutrient reference: energy 367 kcal/100g, protein 3.5g, fat 15.7g, carbohydrate 55.8g. HPB source is retained in provenance notes but its per-100g values are superseded by USDA data for nutritional integrity.

gi_source.title: Chocolate cake, made from packet mix with frosting — Atkinson et al. (2008), International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 38 (low) is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for chocolate cake made from packet mix with frosting (Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008;31:2281–2283). This is the closest approved analogous entry for a classic chocolate cake. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (38) × carbohydrate per serving (55.8g × 0.80kg serving / 100) / 100 = 38 × 44.64 / 100 ≈ 16.9 per 80g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (35.5g/100g), moderate sodium (299mg/100g), and high refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Classic chocolate cake should be strictly limited for individuals with hypertension; its high sugar content independently elevates blood pressure risk (AHA 2018), and its sodium content of 299mg/100g contributes meaningfully to daily sodium load — limit to occasional small portions only.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (35.5g/100g), high refined carbohydrate (55.8g/100g), and high glycemic load per serving (~16.9)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Classic chocolate cake is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; despite a moderate GI of 38, the high carbohydrate and sugar density produce a high glycemic load per serving (~16.9), causing significant postprandial glucose excursions — avoid or limit to very small portions with careful carbohydrate budgeting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (4.6g/100g), trans fat (0.1g/100g), high sugar, and high caloric density (367 kcal/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Classic chocolate cake poses a high cardiovascular concern due to its saturated fat, trans fat, and high added-sugar content, all of which contribute to dyslipidaemia and endothelial dysfunction — individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Classic Chocolate Cake (Whole) is a baked Western-style dessert cake consisting of layered chocolate sponge or butter cake filled and coated with chocolate frosting or ganache. The 6-inch whole cake format is commonly sold in Singapore bakeries and supermarkets as a celebration or everyday dessert. The cake is made from wheat flour, cocoa powder, sugar, butter or vegetable shortening, eggs, milk, and leavening agents, then baked and assembled with chocolate buttercream or fudge frosting. The texture is moist and dense with a rich, sweet chocolate flavour. Per 100g, it provides approximately 367 kcal, 3.5g protein, 15.7g fat (of which 4.6g is saturated), and 55.8g carbohydrate including 35.5g sugar. Sodium is 299mg/100g. The glycemic index is approximately 38 (low category per University of Sydney GI Database), but the glycemic load per standard 80g slice is high (~16.9) due to the large carbohydrate portion. Clinically, this food is high concern for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar, saturated fat, trans fat, and caloric density. It is not suitable for regular consumption in cardiometabolic disease management. It is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore retail contexts. Common allergens include gluten, eggs, and dairy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of chocolate cake?
Is chocolate cake suitable for diabetics?
Is chocolate cake high in sugar?
Can I eat chocolate cake if I have high blood pressure?
Classic chocolate cake whole Singapore calories

confidence_reason: The Singapore HPB source record for this item contains anomalously low macronutrient values (42 kcal/100g, 0g protein, 0.25g fat) that are inconsistent with any known chocolate cake formulation and appear to represent a data entry error or a different food category entirely. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 18101) for commercially prepared chocolate cake with frosting is used as the authoritative nutrient reference, representing the closest approved-source match. Confidence is medium because the exact Singapore retail formulation was not directly matched in an approved source.
notes: 1. HPB source anomaly: The input HPB per-100g values (energy 42 kcal, protein 0g, fat 0.25g, carbohydrate 10.36g, sugar 9.94g) are nutritionally implausible for a whole chocolate cake and have been superseded by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data (FDC ID 18101) for nutritional integrity. The HPB values may represent a flavoured syrup, beverage, or data entry error. 2. Protein_g: USDA reports 3.5g/100g; protein is present from eggs, milk, and wheat flour in the cake formulation — not zero. 3. Fat_g: USDA reports 15.7g/100g from butter/shortening and egg yolks — not zero. 4. Vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: detailed vitamin D data not available in USDA SR Legacy entry for this product. 5. Vitamin_e_mg set to -1: detailed vitamin E data not available in USDA SR Legacy entry for this product. 6. Selenium_mcg set to -1: selenium data not reported in USDA SR Legacy entry for this product. 7. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (chocolate cake from packet mix with frosting); no direct GI measurement for this specific Singapore retail product was found in approved sources. 8. The input health_suitability entries referenced 'sugar-sweetened beverage' cautions which are not applicable to a whole cake; these have been replaced with clinically accurate cake-specific guidance.
